readerView.agentInstruction: Do not read, parse, or update this HTML file during normal task work. Agents coordinate by updating board.json only.

transitionApi.apiLog: task-board/task-board-api.log records every GET and POST API request as JSON Lines with timestamp, method, endpoint, task ID, agentName, status, and error details.
transitionApi.agentInstruction: When the local task-board backend is running, prefer these endpoints for task creation, edits, status changes, and cleanup so claims and moves are atomic. If the backend is not running, update board.json directly using the same workflow rules.
transitionApi.agentName: Every agent must register at chat start with POST /api/register-agent (personalName, model, role) to receive an agentId, then include that agentId in every later API payload.
transitionApi.titleValidation: Every task must have a real, nonblank title. Untitled, Untitled task, and blank titles are invalid; the API rejects them and the viewer hides malformed records.

roleSeparation.planner: Planner is planning-only. It records requirements in board.json, but never switches into Worker or Reviewer behavior in the same chat.
roleSeparation.worker: Worker execution starts only in a separate Worker chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Worker role.
roleSeparation.reviewer: Reviewer review starts only in a separate Reviewer chat or when the owner explicitly starts an agent with the Reviewer role.
roleSeparation.hardRule: Planner must not review completed work, claim tasks, edit product code, run implementation, or make code changes. Reviewer may update only board.json (plus reference images when recorded on a follow-up task). Reviewer must not plan general work, claim todo tasks, edit code, edit docs, edit the HTML viewer, run implementation, or make code changes.

claimed.title: Show version and branch in frontend corner
claimed.summary: Add a small, unobtrusive frontend indicator that shows the current app version and git branch in a corner of the interface.
requirements: Display both the repository version and current git branch in a fixed corner location of the frontend interface.
Use the existing VERSION file as the source for the version value.
Use the current git branch as the branch value when available, with a graceful fallback such as unknown when it cannot be determined.
Keep the indicator visually subtle and ensure it does not block or overlap primary controls, task content, or small-screen navigation.
If both viewer frontends are actively used, keep the visible version/branch treatment consistent across them.
acceptanceCriteria: The frontend shows a corner label containing version and branch values, including branch feature/orchia-organic-v3 when run from the current branch.
The version value matches the contents of VERSION without hardcoding a stale copy into the UI.
The indicator remains readable on desktop and mobile viewports and does not interfere with existing interactions.
The implementation handles missing git metadata without breaking page rendering.
Worker notes identify the URL or file path used to inspect the completed UI.

inspectionTargets.label: Task board viewer
inspectionTargets.state: Start the task-board backend and open the viewer normally.
inspectionTargets.notes: Confirm the version/branch indicator appears in a corner and does not cover task board controls.

claimed.notes: The repository was on branch feature/orchia-organic-v3 when this task was created.
